finding.recommendation: Keep all 8 /deep:* commands stable and repoint only skill paths to deep-loop-workflows; make /deep:ask-ai-council the canonical council command and do not reintroduce /speckit:deep-council into live command assets; keep per-mode YAML workflows plus an optional thin mode/asset manifest, and keep loop-host.cjs as an improvement-lane runner rather than extending it to context/research/review/council.
key_tradeoffs: Stable commands preserve behavior and operator muscle memory but retain 8 public entrypoints
Per-mode YAML preserves byte-identical artifacts but leaves duplicated gate/presentation structure
Lane-local loop-host avoids behavior rewrites but means the merged skill is a package/router, not one executable host
risks: Mechanical skill-path misses in YAML, presentation assets, or inline loop-host command snippets
Confusing command :auto/:confirm, runtime --loop-type, and loop-host --mode layers
Accidentally changing per-mode artifacts by reimplementing YAML orchestration in Node
External users may remember historical /speckit:deep-council even though live command assets now use /deep:ask-ai-council
depends_on: Q-ARCH internal layout old subtrees vs modes/<mode>
open_questions: Does any external/non-repo surface still require a /speckit:deep-council compatibility alias?
Will the merged skill preserve old subtree paths or normalize to modes/<mode>/?
